frameworks.name: Structured Event Memory (SEM)
frameworks.one_line: neuro-symbolic generative model of event segmentation, schemata, and memory reconstruction

frameworks.name: probabilistic generative model of event dynamics
frameworks.one_line: scene dynamics formulated probabilistically over structured symbolic representations

frameworks.name: symbolic structured representation
frameworks.one_line: discrete relational scene descriptions combined with continuous embeddings

frameworks.name: neural network embeddings
frameworks.one_line: vector-space parametrization of scene dynamics for high-dimensional inputs

core_question: How do humans segment continuous experience into discrete events and use learned event structure to organize memory and generalize?
key_assumptions: experience can be modeled as discrete event units with learnable structure
scenes can be represented symbolically and embedded in a vector space
event boundaries and schemata arise from probabilistic inference
memory reconstruction uses event knowledge as prior
manipulations: structured symbolic vs. continuous neural representations of scenes
scaling input dimensionality up to naturalistic video
key_findings: SEM infers event boundaries from probabilistic reasoning over the generative model
model learns event schemata and reconstructs past experience using them
produces human-like event segmentation on naturalistic video data
accounts for a wide array of memory phenomena
interpretations: combining structured symbolic and neural network approaches captures high-level event cognition
event segmentation, schema learning, and memory reconstruction emerge from a single generative model
